title: Quiz: Quantum Layers
description: Test your understanding of quantum layer architectures including hardware-efficient, strongly entangling, and symmetry-preserving designs

questions.question: What is the primary design principle of hardware-efficient ansatze?

options.text: Match the native gates and connectivity of the quantum device

questions.explanation: Hardware-efficient ansatze are designed to use native gate sets and respect the connectivity constraints of the target quantum hardware, enabling shallow circuits with reduced error.

questions.question: In a strongly entangling layer with n qubits, how many CZ gates are used for all-to-all connectivity?
questions.explanation: All-to-all connectivity requires a CZ gate between every pair of qubits. The number of pairs is n choose 2, which equals n(n-1)/2.

questions.question: What does a symmetry-preserving ansatz guarantee?

options.text: The quantum state remains within a specific symmetry sector

questions.explanation: Symmetry-preserving ansatze ensure that if a state starts in a symmetry sector (e.g., fixed particle number), it remains there throughout the circuit evolution.

questions.question: Increasing circuit depth always improves both expressibility and trainability.
questions.explanation: While increased depth generally improves expressibility, it can lead to barren plateaus where gradients vanish exponentially, making training extremely difficult.

questions.question: Which entanglement pattern does a hardware-efficient ansatz typically use?
questions.explanation: Hardware-efficient ansatze use linear (nearest-neighbor) entanglement patterns because they can be directly implemented on most quantum hardware without SWAP operations.

questions.question: What is expressibility in the context of quantum layers?

options.text: The number of parameters in the circuit
How uniformly the circuit can sample quantum states

questions.explanation: Expressibility measures how well a parameterized quantum circuit can explore the space of quantum states, ideally matching the uniform (Haar) distribution.

questions.question: For quantum chemistry applications, which type of ansatz is most appropriate?
questions.explanation: Quantum chemistry requires conserving the number of electrons (particles). Particle-number conserving ansatze respect this physical constraint, reducing the search space.

questions.question: Which are valid considerations when choosing a quantum layer architecture? (Select all)

options.text: The color scheme of the circuit diagram

questions.explanation: Layer architecture should consider hardware connectivity (a), problem symmetries (b), and the expressibility-trainability trade-off (c). Visual presentation is irrelevant to circuit performance.
